wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started content for LineCheck, an interview preparation and job application assistant tool. Explain the project's purpose as a React-based frontend application with serverless backend capabilities. Document installation requirements including Node.js, npm, and browser compatibility. Provide step-by-step setup instructions for development environment, including running the development server, building for production, and basic usage examples. Include quick start tutorial covering core features like question generation, job application management, and text-to-speech functionality. Address common setup issues and provide troubleshooting guidance for beginners.

wiki_catalogs.name: UI Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for LineCheck's core UI components. Document BrandLogo component for brand identity display, DocumentField component for form input handling, FocusBubbles component for visual focus indicators, GenerateLoading component for loading states, and InstallPrompt component for PWA installation prompts. For each component, provide comprehensive prop specifications, event handlers, styling customization options, accessibility features, and responsive design patterns. Include practical usage examples with code snippets demonstrating proper implementation and integration patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Interview Question Generator
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Interview Question Generator feature. Explain how users can generate AI-powered interview questions based on job descriptions, candidate profiles, and interview modes. Document the question generation algorithms, customization options including difficulty levels and question types, and integration with the candidate management system. Include practical examples of different interview scenarios, prompt engineering techniques, and how to configure mandatory questions. Detail the QaList component functionality for managing generated questions, editing capabilities, and export options.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive system design documentation for LineCheck's modern React SPA architecture. Explain the high-level system boundaries, technology stack decisions, and architectural patterns used throughout the application. Document the separation between frontend React components and backend serverless APIs. Detail the build system using Vite, deployment strategy on Vercel, and progressive web app capabilities. Include system context diagrams showing component interactions, external service integrations (Edge TTS, AI chat), and data flow patterns. Address scalability considerations, performance optimization strategies, and security boundaries between client and server components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Internationalization (i18n)
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for LineCheck's internationalization system. Document the i18n module architecture, language management, translation loading mechanisms, and context-based translation usage. Explain how to add new languages, manage translation keys, handle pluralization and formatting, and implement dynamic language switching. Include examples of using the I18nContext in React components, best practices for organizing translation files, and performance optimization strategies for large translation datasets.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Environment Setup
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development environment setup documentation for LineCheck. Document the complete local development workflow including Node.js requirements, dependency installation with npm/yarn, and project initialization steps. Explain Vite configuration options, development server setup, and hot reloading capabilities. Cover the custom API development server using dev-api-server.mjs, including how to run it alongside the frontend. Document environment variables, configuration files, and IDE setup recommendations. Include troubleshooting common setup issues and performance tips for optimal development experience.

wiki_catalogs.name: Chat API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the Chat endpoint. Document the HTTP POST method, request/response schemas for AI conversations, message formats, and conversation state management. Include examples of sending messages, receiving AI responses, handling conversation context, and managing chat sessions. Document error codes, rate limiting, authentication requirements, and security considerations. Provide practical examples with curl commands and JavaScript fetch calls demonstrating common use cases like question generation, interview preparation, and content analysis.

wiki_catalogs.name: Question Generation Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Question Generation Engine. Explain how the AI-powered question generation system works, including prompt engineering techniques, conversation flow management, and integration with the chat API. Document the different types of questions generated (technical, behavioral, situational), customization parameters like difficulty levels and focus areas, and how the system adapts to different job roles and experience levels. Include examples of effective prompts, response parsing logic, and error handling strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for LineCheck's modern React SPA with serverless backend architecture. Describe the high-level system design using React components, Vite build system, and Edge Functions deployment. Document the component hierarchy starting from App.jsx through Shell to page components. Explain the separation between frontend React application and backend API services. Detail the data flow patterns, state management approach using Context API, and integration with external services like Edge TTS. Include technology stack overview, directory structure explanation, and deployment topology on Vercel. Address performance considerations, caching strategies, and progressive web app capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Layout Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for LineCheck's layout and data presentation components. Document QaList component for displaying question-answer pairs with interactive features, sorting, and filtering capabilities. Document Shell component as the main application layout wrapper providing navigation, routing, and global state management. Include comprehensive prop specifications, event handling patterns, data binding examples, and integration with the application's state management system. Address responsive design considerations, accessibility compliance, and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Job Application Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the Job Application Management system. Explain how users can track job applications, manage application status, and organize their job search workflow. Document the settings configuration interface, data persistence mechanisms using local storage, and job metadata management. Include examples of application tracking workflows, status management patterns, and data import/export functionality. Address user preferences, application categories, and reporting features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed component architecture documentation for LineCheck's React-based UI structure. Document the component hierarchy starting from App.jsx as the root component through Shell.jsx layout wrapper to page components (HomePage, SettingsPage). Explain the component composition patterns, prop drilling vs Context API usage, and state management approach. Detail the relationship between UI components (BrandLogo, QaList, FocusBubbles) and their responsibilities. Include component lifecycle patterns, event handling strategies, and communication patterns between parent-child and sibling components. Provide examples of reusable component patterns and best practices for component organization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Storage Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for LineCheck's storage management utility. Document local storage operations, data persistence patterns, key-value storage interface, and data serialization/deserialization. Explain storage quotas, error handling for storage failures, data migration strategies, and backup/restore functionality. Include examples of storing complex objects, handling storage limits, and implementing data versioning. Address security considerations for sensitive data storage and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing Strategy and Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed testing documentation for LineCheck covering the complete testing strategy and implementation. Document Playwright-based end-to-end testing setup, test file organization, and test execution workflows. Explain the TTS regression testing framework using tts_regression.mjs, including how to run tests, interpret results, and maintain test suites. Cover unit testing approaches for React components and utility functions. Document API testing strategies for backend endpoints, mocking techniques, and integration testing patterns. Include test data management, continuous integration setup, and debugging failed tests effectively.

wiki_catalogs.name: Text-to-Speech API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for the Text-to-Speech endpoints. Document HTTP methods for audio generation, voice configuration parameters, text input formats, and audio output specifications. Include examples of different voice options, language settings, speech rates, and audio format selections. Document shared utilities used across TTS endpoints, error handling for invalid inputs, and performance optimization tips. Provide practical examples with curl commands and JavaScript implementations for generating speech from text content.

wiki_catalogs.name: Candidate Profile Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for Candidate Profile Integration. Detail how candidate data structures are managed, including profile fields, experience tracking, skill assessment, and application status. Document the storage mechanisms, data validation rules, and synchronization between local storage and UI components. Explain how candidate information influences question generation, interview mode selection, and progress tracking. Include examples of candidate data models, import/export functionality, and data migration patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for LineCheck's core features including interview question generation, job application management, text-to-speech integration, and document processing. Explain how users can generate AI-powered interview questions, manage job applications with status tracking, utilize text-to-speech for audio playback, and export documents to PDF format. Document the user workflows, feature interactions, and configuration options available to users. Include practical examples demonstrating common use cases and integration patterns between different features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Text-to-Speech Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Text-to-Speech integration system. Explain how the TTS service provides audio playback for interview questions and content. Document the Edge TTS integration, voice configuration options, audio format support, and performance optimization strategies. Include API reference for both frontend and backend components, error handling patterns, and health check endpoints. Provide examples of implementing TTS in custom components and troubleshooting common audio playback issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Flow Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data flow documentation for LineCheck's state management and data handling patterns. Document the unidirectional data flow from user interactions through component state to storage persistence. Explain the Context API implementation for global state management including I18nContext for internationalization. Detail local storage patterns for persistent data like candidate information and settings configuration. Document data transformation pipelines, validation flows, and error handling strategies. Include examples of async data fetching patterns, caching mechanisms, and synchronization between client state and external APIs. Address data consistency, optimistic updates, and conflict resolution strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Document Processing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for LineCheck's document processing utilities covering PDF export and OCR functionality. Document the PDF generation API, template customization, layout options, and export formats. Explain OCR integration for image-to-text conversion, supported file formats, accuracy settings, and preprocessing options. Include examples of generating interview documents, customizing PDF templates, handling large documents, and optimizing OCR performance. Address error handling, fallback mechanisms, and browser compatibility considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Build Process and Deployment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive build and deployment documentation for LineCheck. Document the Vite build process, optimization settings, and production bundle generation. Explain Vercel deployment configuration using vercel.json, including environment variables, routing rules, and edge function deployment. Cover Progressive Web App build considerations, service worker configuration, and asset optimization. Document CI/CD pipeline setup, automated testing in deployment, and rollback procedures. Include performance monitoring, error tracking integration, and production debugging techniques. Address scaling considerations and infrastructure requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: TTS Health Check API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the TTS Health Check endpoint. Document the GET method for monitoring TTS service availability, response formats indicating service status, and integration patterns for health monitoring systems. Include examples of health check requests, response interpretation, and troubleshooting steps when the service is unavailable. Document retry strategies and fallback mechanisms for production deployments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Interview Modes Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Interview Modes Configuration. Explain the different interview modes available (technical, behavioral, case study, etc.), their characteristics, and when to use each mode. Document how mandatory questions are integrated into different interview scenarios, customization options for question sequences, and adaptive questioning based on user responses. Include configuration examples, mode-specific algorithms, and how to extend the system with new interview modes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Library
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for LineCheck's React component library. Document each UI component including BrandLogo, DocumentField, FocusBubbles, GenerateLoading, InstallPrompt, QaList, and Shell components. For each component, describe props/attributes, events, styling options, and usage examples. Include component composition patterns, accessibility features, and responsive design considerations. Provide code snippets demonstrating proper implementation and integration with other components. Address customization options and theming support.

wiki_catalogs.name: Document Processing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the Document Processing capabilities including PDF export functionality and OCR (Optical Character Recognition) integration. Explain how users can export interview questions and job application data to PDF format, process uploaded documents through OCR, and utilize the DocumentField component for file input. Document supported file formats, export templates, OCR accuracy considerations, and performance optimization for large document processing. Include examples of customizing PDF layouts and handling various document types.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed deployment architecture documentation for LineCheck's serverless deployment on Vercel. Document the build process using Vite, static asset optimization, and progressive web app configuration. Explain the Edge Functions architecture for API endpoints, serverless function deployment, and cold start optimization. Detail the service worker implementation for offline capabilities, caching strategies, and background sync. Include development server setup, environment configuration, and deployment pipeline automation. Address performance monitoring, error tracking, and scaling considerations for production deployments. Document CDN usage, asset optimization, and browser compatibility strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Network Utilities
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for LineCheck's network utility functions. Document URL fetching capabilities, request/response handling, error management, and security considerations. Explain content extraction from URLs, caching strategies, timeout handling, and retry mechanisms. Include examples of fetching external content, handling different response types, implementing rate limiting, and managing CORS issues. Address security best practices for external content fetching and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: URL Fetch API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the URL Fetch endpoint. Document HTTP methods for retrieving external content, supported URL formats, request headers, and response processing. Include security considerations for external content fetching, CORS policies, and content validation. Document error handling for network failures, timeout scenarios, and invalid URLs. Provide practical examples with curl commands and JavaScript implementations for fetching web content, documents, and structured data from external sources.

wiki_catalogs.name: Question List Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for Question List Management. Detail the QaList component functionality including question display, editing capabilities, reordering, filtering, and search operations. Document CRUD operations for questions, batch operations, export formats (PDF, text), and integration with the storage system. Explain user interactions, keyboard navigation, accessibility features, and responsive design considerations. Include examples of list customization, bulk operations, and performance optimization for large question sets.

wiki_catalogs.name: Utility Libraries
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for LineCheck's utility libraries and helper modules. Document internationalization (i18n) system, storage management, PDF export functionality, OCR processing, URL fetching utilities, interview modes configuration, job metadata handling, mandatory questions management, and settings configuration. For each utility, explain the API surface, configuration options, usage patterns, and integration examples. Include error handling strategies, performance considerations, and best practices for extending functionality.

wiki_catalogs.name: Interview Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for LineCheck's interview configuration utilities. Document interview modes system, job metadata handling, and mandatory questions management. Explain how to configure different interview types, customize question sets, manage job-specific parameters, and implement dynamic question generation. Include examples of adding new interview modes, extending job metadata schemas, configuring mandatory question rules, and integrating with the question generation engine. Address validation rules, default configurations, and extensibility patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for LineCheck's serverless backend endpoints. Document the Chat API for AI conversations, Text-to-Speech API for audio generation, TTS Health Check endpoint, and URL Fetch API for external content retrieval. For each endpoint, specify HTTP methods, request/response schemas, authentication requirements, error codes, and rate limiting. Include practical examples with curl commands and JavaScript fetch calls. Document shared utilities, error handling patterns, and security considerations. Address versioning strategy and backwards compatibility.

wiki_catalogs.name: Settings Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for LineCheck's settings configuration system. Document settings schema definition, validation rules, default values, and runtime configuration management. Explain how to add new settings, implement setting migrations, handle configuration updates, and manage user preferences. Include examples of defining complex settings structures, implementing conditional logic, validating user input, and persisting configuration changes. Address settings synchronization across devices and conflict resolution strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration & Customization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for LineCheck's configuration options and customization capabilities. Document application settings including theme customization, language preferences, TTS configuration, and user preferences management. Explain environment variables, build-time configuration, and runtime settings. Cover Progressive Web App manifest configuration, deployment settings for Vercel, and linting rules. Include examples of common customizations, extension points for developers, and migration guides for configuration changes. Address security implications and best practices for configuration management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development documentation for LineCheck contributors and developers. Document the development workflow including local setup, hot reloading, debugging techniques, and testing strategies. Explain the build process using Vite, development server configuration, and API mocking. Cover code style guidelines, linting rules, and contribution processes. Include scripts documentation for development utilities, regression testing, and automation tasks. Address performance optimization, bundle analysis, and production deployment procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting & FAQ
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting documentation for LineCheck addressing common issues, errors, and their solutions. Document browser compatibility issues, performance problems, and debugging techniques. Include frequently asked questions about setup, configuration, and feature usage. Address Progressive Web App issues, service worker problems, and offline functionality troubleshooting. Provide diagnostic tools, logging approaches, and error reporting mechanisms. Include migration guides for updates and backwards compatibility notes.
